content: read reviews/584.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/584.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/584.md.

    10→
    11→## 3. Module Docstring
    12→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–29).
    13→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 584`.
    14→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/584`.
    15→- [~] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem. **Minor note:** The module docstring body is just "A problem of Erdős, Duke, and Rödl." — this is an attribution, not a description of what the problem asks. The individual theorem docstrings do provide detailed descriptions, so this is acceptable but could be improved by adding a one-sentence summary of the problem (e.g., "Concerns the existence of dense subgraphs in which every pair of edges lies on a short common cycle.").
    16→- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: `[DuEr82]` and `[DER84]`.
    17→
    18→## 4. Namespace
    19→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos584 ... end Erdos584` (lines 33–92).
    20→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos584`.
    21→- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block (line 31).
    22→
    23→## 5. Custom Definitions
    24→- [x] `EdgesOnCommonCycle` has a docstring: `/-- Two edges of a graph lie on a common cycle of length at most $k$. -/` (line 35).
    25→- [x] `EdgesShareVertex` has a docstring: `/-- Two edges share a vertex. -/` (line 41).
    26→- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (both definitions are `Prop`-valued with no classical choice).
    27→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    28→- [x] Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `EdgesOnCommonCycle`, `EdgesShareVertex`.
    29→- [x] No problem-specific notation needed.
    30→- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions. **Optional but recommended** — a quick test that `EdgesOnCommonCycle` and `EdgesShareVertex` behave as expected on small examples would increase confidence.
    31→
    32→## 6. Theorem Statements
    33→- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword (lines 55, 79).
    34→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_584` (line 55).
    35→- [x] Name follows `snake_case` conventions: `erdos_584`, `erdos_584.variants.part2`.
    36→- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]` (lines 54, 78).
    37→- [x] Main theorem and variant both have docstrings (lines 45–53, 70–77).
    38→- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: `[DuEr82, DER84]`.
    39→
    40→### `answer()` usage
    41→- [x] **Case 3 applies.** The original problem is a direct assertion ("there exists a subgraph H with these properties"), not a yes/no question or a find-the-value problem. Accordingly, no `answer()` wrapper is used — the theorem type is `P` directly. This is correct.
    42→- [x] No quantifiers that should be inside the statement are instead placed as theorem parameters — all quantifiers (`∀ n, ∀ δ, ∀ G`) are properly inside the theorem body.
    43→
    44→## 7. Category Attribute
    45→- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
    46→- [x] Value is `research open` — correct per the source (erdosproblems.com lists it as open).
    47→- [x] Both `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.
    48→
    49→## 8. AMS Attribute
    50→- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
    51→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
    52→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number, trivially satisfied).
    53→- [x] No duplicates.
    54→- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics — appropriate for this graph theory problem).
    55→
    56→## 9. Proof Bodies
    57→- [x] Open conjectures use multi-line `by\n  sorry` format (lines 67–68, 89–90). Acceptable per checklist.
    58→- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
    59→- [x] No informal proof sketches for open problems.
    60→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    61→
    62→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    63→- [x] Variant named `erdos_584.variants.part2` (line 79) — follows `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` convention.
    64→- [x] Variant lives in the same file as the main theorem.
    65→- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research open, AMS 5]` attribute (line 78).
    66→- [x] Variant has a detailed docstring distinguishing it from the main theorem (Part 2: `δ² n²` edges, cycle length ≤ 8, vs Part 1: `δ³ n²` edges, cycle length ≤ 6 + sharing-vertex condition).
    67→
    68→## 11. Code Quality
    69→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    70→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `→`, `≥`, `≤`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`.
    71→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    72→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    73→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    74→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline after `end Erdos584`.
    75→
    76→## 12. Build Verification
    77→- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review. Structure and syntax appear correct; no obvious errors.
    78→- [ ] Custom linter compliance not machine-verified. Based on manual inspection, all five linters should pass.
    79→
    80→---
    81→
    82→## Summary
    83→
    84→**Verdict: PASS** (with minor notes)
    85→
    86→The file is well-structured and conforms to essentially all checklist requirements. The formalization correctly captures the two-part problem: Part 1 asks for a subgraph with `≫ δ³n²` edges where all edge pairs share a cycle of length ≤ 6 (and sharing-vertex pairs share one of length ≤ 4), while Part 2 relaxes the cycle bound to ≤ 8 and the edge density to `δ²n²` but drops the sharing-vertex condition. Both are correctly classified as `research open` with AMS code 5 (Combinatorics).
    87→
    88→**Minor observations:**
    89→1. The module docstring body could include a brief description of the problem content (not just attribution).
    90→2. Optional sanity-check tests (`@[category test]`) for the helper definitions `EdgesOnCommonCycle` and `EdgesShareVertex` are not present.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/584.lean

## 1. Copyright Header
- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement
- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` on line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring
- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–29).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 584`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/584`.
- [~] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem. **Minor note:** The module docstring body is just "A problem of Erdős, Duke, and Rödl." — this is an attribution, not a description of what the problem asks. The individual theorem docstrings do provide detailed descriptions, so this is acceptable but could be improved by adding a one-sentence summary of the problem (e.g., "Concerns the existence of dense subgraphs in which every pair of edges lies on a short common cycle.").
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: `[DuEr82]` and `[DER84]`.

## 4. Namespace
- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos584 ... end Erdos584` (lines 33–92).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos584`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block (line 31).

## 5. Custom Definitions
- [x] `EdgesOnCommonCycle` has a docstring: `/-- Two edges of a graph lie on a common cycle of length at most $k$. -/` (line 35).
- [x] `EdgesShareVertex` has a docstring: `/-- Two edges share a vertex. -/` (line 41).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (both definitions are `Prop`-valued with no classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `EdgesOnCommonCycle`, `EdgesShareVertex`.
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions. **Optional but recommended** — a quick test that `EdgesOnCommonCycle` and `EdgesShareVertex` behave as expected on small examples would increase confidence.

## 6. Theorem Statements
- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword (lines 55, 79).
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_584` (line 55).
- [x] Name follows `snake_case` conventions: `erdos_584`, `erdos_584.variants.part2`.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]` (lines 54, 78).
- [x] Main theorem and variant both have docstrings (lines 45–53, 70–77).
- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: `[DuEr82, DER84]`.

### `answer()` usage
- [x] **Case 3 applies.** The original problem is a direct assertion ("there exists a subgraph H with these properties"), not a yes/no question or a find-the-value problem. Accordingly, no `answer()` wrapper is used — the theorem type is `P` directly. This is correct.
- [x] No quantifiers that should be inside the statement are instead placed as theorem parameters — all quantifiers (`∀ n, ∀ δ, ∀ G`) are properly inside the theorem body.

## 7. Category Attribute
- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Value is `research open` — correct per the source (erdosproblems.com lists it as open).
- [x] Both `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.

## 8. AMS Attribute
- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics — appropriate for this graph theory problem).

## 9. Proof Bodies
- [x] Open conjectures use multi-line `by\n  sorry` format (lines 67–68, 89–90). Acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
- [x] Variant named `erdos_584.variants.part2` (line 79) — follows `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` convention.
- [x] Variant lives in the same file as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research open, AMS 5]` attribute (line 78).
- [x] Variant has a detailed docstring distinguishing it from the main theorem (Part 2: `δ² n²` edges, cycle length ≤ 8, vs Part 1: `δ³ n²` edges, cycle length ≤ 6 + sharing-vertex condition).

## 11. Code Quality
- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `→`, `≥`, `≤`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline after `end Erdos584`.

## 12. Build Verification
- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review. Structure and syntax appear correct; no obvious errors.
- [ ] Custom linter compliance not machine-verified. Based on manual inspection, all five linters should pass.

---

## Summary

**Verdict: PASS** (with minor notes)

The file is well-structured and conforms to essentially all checklist requirements. The formalization correctly captures the two-part problem: Part 1 asks for a subgraph with `≫ δ³n²` edges where all edge pairs share a cycle of length ≤ 6 (and sharing-vertex pairs share one of length ≤ 4), while Part 2 relaxes the cycle bound to ≤ 8 and the edge density to `δ²n²` but drops the sharing-vertex condition. Both are correctly classified as `research open` with AMS code 5 (Combinatorics).

**Minor observations:**
1. The module docstring body could include a brief description of the problem content (not just attribution).
2. Optional sanity-check tests (`@[category test]`) for the helper definitions `EdgesOnCommonCycle` and `EdgesShareVertex` are not present.

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
